response:
to find the denominator we must store the prime factors of one hundred factorial
***
the number ten to the power fifty holds many factors of two and five to store
***
we store the count of fives in one hundred factorial which is twenty five
***
we store the count of twos in one hundred factorial which is ninety six
***
after dividing by ten to the power fifty we store the remaining twos as fifty one
***
the five factors are completely removed so we store zero fives in the result
***
since the fraction is irreducible the denominator must store only the remaining prime
***
the only prime left in the denominator is two raised to the power fifty one
***
we store this final value as the answer to the mathematical puzzle posed
***
the denominator is two to the fifty first power which we have stored
